app.name: Cerul
app.one_liner: The video search layer for AI agents that indexes speech, visuals, and on-screen text to enable semantic search over video content.

screens.name: Admin Analytics Dashboard
screens.purpose: Monitor search performance, user engagement, and content effectiveness through impression-aware CTR metrics and leaderboards.
primary_actions: View top queries and empty result rates

screens.name: API Playground
screens.purpose: Enable developers to test video search queries, view results with previews, and generate client code in multiple languages.

rust_commands.name: generate_code_snippet
rust_commands.purpose: Produce syntax-highlighted client code for API usage in supported languages.

rust_commands.name: search_video
rust_commands.purpose: Execute a multimodal semantic search over indexed video content using natural language queries.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build basic playground UI with query input and result preview
week 1: Deploy minimal Cloudflare Worker API with /search endpoint
week 1: Implement core multimodal indexing pipeline using Gemini for visual and speech analysis
week 2: Add syntax-highlighted code generation for Python/JS/Shell/Go
week 2: Implement short_id-based redirect URLs for retrieval units
week 2: Integrate thumbs up/down feedback collection
week 3: Add collapsible advanced search parameters
week 3: Develop admin analytics dashboard with CTR and query performance views
week 3: Publish npm and PyPI client packages
acceptance_tests: Admin analytics correctly compute impression-aware CTR by tracking both result views and clicks per query and position.
After submitting a search, the system logs a snapshot of results in query_logs.results_preview, enabling replay and audit even if underlying video metadata changes.
Given a natural language query about visual content (e.g., 'show me videos with Python code'), the system returns video segments containing actual code visuals, not just matching transcripts.
When a developer uses the playground, they can copy generated Python code that successfully executes against the live API and returns the same results shown in the UI.
